question: A FEEL expression `date and time(@"2020-07-31T14:27:30@Europe/Berlin", "America/Los_Angeles")` is evaluated. Which statement about `date and time(date, timezone)` is NOT correct?

options.text: It is a Camunda extension to standard FEEL.
When the input has a different timezone than the target, the wall-clock time is adjusted to the local time of the target timezone.
It simply re-labels the supplied timestamp with the new timezone without changing the underlying instant or the wall-clock time.
The result is a `date and time` value carrying the target timezone.

a.text: Correct as stated; the docs mark this overload with the Camunda extension marker, so it is a Camunda addition rather than standard FEEL.

b.text: Correct as stated; the docs example converts `14:27:30@Europe/Berlin` to `05:27:30@America/Los_Angeles`, showing that the wall-clock time is adjusted to the local time of the target zone.

c.text: This is the false statement — and therefore the answer to a negative question. The function adjusts the wall-clock time, as shown when `14:27:30@Europe/Berlin` becomes `05:27:30@America/Los_Angeles`. It does not merely re-label the timezone.

d.text: Correct as stated; the returned value carries the supplied target timezone, e.g. `…@America/Los_Angeles` in the docs example.

explanation: `date and time(date, timezone)` is a Camunda extension that converts a `date and time` value into the supplied timezone, shifting the wall-clock time as needed so the instant remains the same. It does NOT just re-label the timezone while keeping the original wall-clock time.
